description: VoidLink string-based IOCs for Malscope StaticAnalyzer suspicious_indicators

string_groups.description: VoidLink C2 protocol endpoint paths hardcoded in beacon binary
string_groups.note: Any 2 of these paths in a single binary is a strong indicator of VoidLink C2 client code.

string_groups.description: Kernel thread name strings used by VoidLink's prctl masquerade technique
string_groups.note: These names appearing in a user-space binary (not a kernel module) indicate process masquerade. Kernel threads would not contain their own names as string literals.

string_groups.description: VoidStream encryption and camouflage protocol strings
string_groups.note: VoidLink-family specific strings. Negligible false positive rate.

string_groups.description: HTTP camouflage content-type strings used by VoidLink to disguise C2 traffic as image/script/style data
string_groups.note: Multiple HTTP content-type strings in a non-web-server binary indicates camouflage parsing code.

string_groups.description: EDR/security tool names VoidLink enumerates to detect analysis environments
string_groups.note: 3+ security tool names in a binary indicates deliberate EDR enumeration code.

string_groups.description: Strings associated with VoidLink's fileless memory execution technique
string_groups.note: memfd_create + execveat together confirm fileless execution capability.

string_groups.description: VoidLink adaptive beaconing interval strings (aggressive: 4096ms, paranoid: 1024ms)
string_groups.note: Beaconing profile names present in binary indicate C2 communication scheduling code.
TracerPid in /proc/self/status is specifically used by VoidLink for anti-debugging detection.

string_groups.description: AES-256-GCM encryption implementation strings (VoidStream protocol encryption)

scoring.description: Composite IOC scoring for Malscope suspicious_indicators output
scoring.note: Sum the weight of all matched string groups. A score >= 120 should trigger MALICIOUS classification.
